title: T-023 — GS1-128 barcode parser service
prompt:
# T-023 — GS1-128 barcode parser service

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §7.4 (FR-WH-009), §15.2.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
`parseGs1Barcode(input)` shared service handling Application Identifiers (01) GTIN, (10) Lot, (17) Expiry, (11) Manufacture, (37) Count, (310x) Net Weight, (21) Serial.

## Implementation contract
`packages/barcode-parser/src/gs1-parser.ts`. Support FNC1-separated and parentheses-format. Return typed object {gtin?, lot?, expiry?, manufactureDate?, count?, netWeightKg?, serial?} plus `parseErrors[]` and `raw`. SLO <100ms.

## Files (scope)
- packages/barcode-parser/src/gs1-parser.ts [create]
- packages/barcode-parser/src/__tests__/gs1-parser.test.ts [create]
- packages/barcode-parser/package.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given parentheses input '(01)05012345678900(10)B-2026-04-15(17)260501', when parsed, then gtin='05012345678900', lot='B-2026-04-15', expiry=2026-05-01.
2. Given FNC1-separated input with same AIs, when parsed, then the same object is returned.
3. Given (3103)001840 (kg with 3 decimals), when parsed, then netWeightKg equals 1.840.
4. Given malformed input '(99)abc', when parsed, then a parseError row is returned and known fields stay undefined.

## Test strategy
- RED: gs1-parser.test.ts covers four scenarios above plus performance assertion <100ms median.
- Run `pnpm --filter barcode-parser test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not throw on unknown AI — collect into parseErrors.
- Do not import warehouse code from this package.
- Do not depend on Next.js APIs.
